feed.title: Howl Street — Global Financial Wire
feed.subtitle: Loudest Howls and the Pack — auto-curated from 100+ global sources.

entry.title: [LOUDEST HOWL] Trump reviews Iranian proposal aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: The White House confirms Trump met his national security advisers on Monday to discuss the plan.

entry.title: Peso sets new record low, closed at 61.30:$1 #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The Philippine peso finished past the 61-per-dollar level on Tuesday, marking a new record low. This, as the US dollar continues its bullish run amid uncertainties over the Middle East conflict. READ: US dollar still dominates forex markets The local currency weakened by 59 centavos to close at 61.30 against the greenback,

entry.title: SM Prime Q1 bottom line hovers at P11.7B #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — SM Prime Holdings Inc. posted a first-quarter net income of P11.66 billion this year. This was largely flat from the year-ago P11.65 billion, as higher costs offset modest revenue growth. In a disclosure on Tuesday, the Sy family-led property giant said its total revenues rose 2 percent to P33.3 billion. READ: SM

entry.title: LPG business keeps Pryce January-March profit at P1B #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Pryce Corp. posted a profit of P1.084 billion in the first quarter, driven by its strong liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) business. The January-March result was slightly higher than the P1.062 billion recorded in the same period last year. Pryce’s revenues rose by 3.39 percent to P5.98 billion from P5.79 billion. In a

entry.title: Atlas Mining turns around with P645-M profit in Q1 #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: UPDATED WITH QUOTES FROM THE COMPANY MANILA, Philippines — Atlas Consolidated and Mining and Development Corp. reported a net income of P645 million in the first quarter of 2025. This reversed a net loss of P404 million a year ago, driven by higher metal prices. In a disclosure on Tuesday, Atlas Mining said revenues rose

entry.title: Peso touches new low of 60.97:$1 in AM trade; 61.08:$1 in PM session #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: UPDATED FOR AFTERNOON TRADING MANILA, Philippines — The Philippines’s peso slid to a record low on Tuesday, breaching the 61-per-dollar level for the first time in intraday trading READ: Peso depreciates to 60.48:$1 on Middle East war jitters In the afternoon session, the local currency touched an intraday low of 61.08, marking a fresh all-time

entry.title: Apollo Global Capital expands mining business #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Holding firm Apollo Global Capital Inc. has inked a deal with Masbate 2145 Philippines Inc. to significantly expand its presence in the local mining industry. The listed firm signed the operating agreement with Masbate 2145 through its offshore mining subsidiary, JDVC Resources Corp. The agreement covers a 15,909.33-hectare mineral property situated in

entry.title: Ashwini Vaishnaw shares Bullet train plan for South India #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: Proposed high-speed rail network aims to link major cities and create a unified economic region

entry.title: Indonesia stocks lower at close of trade; IDX Composite Index down 0.50% #HowlStreet #Markets

entry.title: Mali's junta can't seem to rely on ally Russia as jihadist offensive threatens regime #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: On Sunday, April 26, after the Azawad Liberation Front rebels seized control of Kidal, at least 400 paramilitaries from Africa Corps were evacuated from the city under armed escort.

entry.title: Euro-Zone Banks Tighten Firms’ Credit Standards Most Since 2023 #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: Euro-zone banks tightened corporate credit standards by the most in more than two years at the start of 2026, as geopolitical events including the Iran war added to an already prevailing trend.

entry.title: Deutsche Börse beats Q1 estimates on broad strength #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: Deutsche Börse beats Q1 estimates on broad strength

entry.title: ETC’s Aerospace and Commercial/Industrial Units Awarded Contracts of Approximately $37 Million #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: SOUTHAMPTON, Pa., April 28, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Environmental Tectonics Corporation (OTC Pink: ETCC) (“ETC” or the “Company”) announced that its Aerospace Solutions Segment has been awarded approximately $31 million in contracts for the delivery of Aircrew Training Systems (“ATS”) to two international customers and included the following Aeromedical products: Four GYRO Integrated Physiological Trainer (GYRO […]

entry.title: Oil Back Above $110 as World Awaits US Response to Iran Proposal #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: Oil rose to a three-week high above $110 a barrel in London as traders awaited the US response to a proposal from Tehran to end the war and reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: Authorities Order Evacuations Following Third Ukrainian Strike on Tuapse Refinery #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: In separate overnight attacks on the Belgorod region, at least three people were killed, regional officials said.

entry.title: China May Restart Fuel Exports as Domestic Stockpiles Surge #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: Chinese state refiners could restart fuel exports next month if the government in Beijing approves their applications for the restart, citing ample domestic stocks. Companies including Sinopec and CNPC have submitted applications for export permits, Bloomberg reported today, citing unnamed sources in the know. The applications are for exports of diesel and gasoline, the Bloomberg sources said. In early March, China told energy companies to suspend new fuel export contracts and try to cancel already arranged fuel shipments abroad as global fuel…
